paper_id: Calhau et al. - 2024 - A System Core Ontology for Capability Emergence Modeling

models.model_label: Well-founded System Core Ontology (UFO-based) for capability emergence modeling

evidence_quotes.quote: In pursuit of an understanding of the emergence phenomenon and the nature of those new kinds of systems in organizations, we propose a well-founded system core ontology based on the Unified Foundational Ontology.
In this context, ontologies are crucial to a better understanding of these systems.
To address this issue, this work aimed at proposing a well-founded system ontology based on Unified Foundational Ontology [20].

goals.justification: The authors explicitly state that the ontology is proposed to support understanding of the emergence phenomenon and the nature of systems, matching the For Understanding signal pattern (understand, conceptualize) as the model's stated purpose.

evidence_quotes.quote: capability emergence models for a particular system (e.g., a specific team), used to understand the emergence for create, change, or analyze it
evidence_quotes.section: Section 5 Capability Emergence Modeling in Enterprise Architecture

evidence_quotes.quote: For example, how should one structure a team into roles in a way that maximizes the overall performance of the team? Or what is the best combination of functions for a team to be more productive?

In this case, for example, the better team's structure (roles and relationships) and professional characteristics (desired capabilities for each role) can be identified from data of the more performative teams (with better KPIs) of an organization.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 5 Capability Emergence Modeling in Enterprise Architecture

goals.justification: The ontology is presented as a means to analyze systems and identify patterns to guide decisions about how to structure teams/roles, matching the For Problem-solving signal pattern (analyze, identify, what should change) without being the implementation instrument itself.

evidence_quotes.quote: The major implication for Enterprise Architecture of this ontology is to provide guidelines for capabilities emergence modeling and emergence pattern identification in EA notations (e.g., ArchiMate).
To improve the representation of this emergence pattern, we created an illustrative language pattern in ArchiMate specific to this case, as shown in Fig. 5.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 6 Emergence in the Spotify Company

evidence_quotes.quote: The use of the system ontology to create language patterns in EA notations will be detailed in the following case study.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 5 Capability Emergence Modeling in Enterprise Architecture

goals.justification: The authors explicitly use the ontology to create a language pattern (artifact) in ArchiMate during the Spotify case study, satisfying the For Intervening rule that the model directly guides creation of an artifact (generated artifact signal).

evidence_quotes.quote: To facilitate the identification and application of the emergence patterns, these tasks can be included into the knowledge management process of the organization. In this case, the identified patterns can even be considered as a knowledge items into the knowledge repository of the organization and forming a 'library' of patterns.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 5 Capability Emergence Modeling in Enterprise Architecture

uncertain_goals.reason: The reference to a knowledge repository/library of patterns describes a possible organizational use of identified patterns rather than the ontology itself being created as documentation; the link to the model's purpose is indirect.
